questions.questionText: An abrasion produced by movement of the body against a rough surface is called:
questions.explanation: Graze or brush abrasion occurs when the skin is rubbed against a rough surface tangentially, causing a scraped area that may indicate direction of force. Pressure abrasion is due to perpendicular compression. Impact abrasion follows a forceful blow. Patterned abrasions reproduce the object design.

questions.questionText: The most characteristic feature of an entry wound caused by a firearm is:
questions.explanation: Typical firearm entry wounds have inverted margins because the projectile pushes skin inward. Exit wounds more often have everted margins. Tailing is more relevant in abrasions. Split lacerations with tissue bridges are blunt force injuries rather than firearm wounds.

questions.questionText: A bruise over the mastoid region seen in basal skull fracture is called:
questions.explanation: Battle sign is postauricular ecchymosis associated with fracture of the base of skull, especially middle cranial fossa. Raccoon eyes are periorbital ecchymosis. Cullen sign is periumbilical ecchymosis. Grey Turner sign is flank ecchymosis, commonly in hemorrhagic pancreatitis.
